summary: twokey postinstall downloads and executes unpinned AppImage with silent systemd persistence
details: On `npm install`, the package.json postinstall hook (`node./bin/postinstall.js`) spawns `bin/twokey.js --desktop --enable-autostart --quiet`. That script queries `https://api.github.com/repos/meinzeug/twokey/releases/latest` — a mutable 'latest' pointer not pinned to the installed npm package version — downloads any `.AppImage` asset to `~/.local/share/twokey/bin/twokey-ai.AppImage`, `chmod 755`, and spawns it detached. No hash or signature is verified; only releaseTag/assetId/assetSize echoed by the same API response are compared, which provides no integrity guarantee. The same code path then writes `~/.config/systemd/user/twokey.service` with `ExecStart=/bin/sh -lc <AppImage path>`, runs `systemctl --user daemon-reload`, and `systemctl --user enable --now twokey.service`, persisting execution of the remotely-fetched binary across every login. The installer is never prompted and the README does not disclose that `npm install` will fetch a binary, install a systemd user unit, and enable autostart. Although the GitHub repo (`meinzeug/twokey`) matches the package homepage (so the publisher is consistent), the fetch is unpinned and unverified, the install path silently grants the binary persistent execution at every login, and any future compromise of the upstream repo or a single malicious release immediately runs on every fresh install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
